content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "database-api",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 35,
    "domain_depth": 52,
    "data_richness": 40,
    "interaction_quality": 38,
    "ai_integration": 30,
    "mobile_first": 25,
    "performance": 48,
    "typescript_quality": 58,
    "error_handling": 55,
    "realtime": 45,
    "accessibility": 20,
    "security": 52,
    "differentiation": 35,
    "enterprise_readiness": 42
  },
  "overall_score": 41,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "database-api-C5-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "No seed data SQL file exists — all pages show empty states",
      "file": "supabase/seed.sql",
      "description": "The supabase/seed/ directory is empty. Every list page (RFIs, submittals, daily logs, punch list, budget, schedule, crews) renders empty states because there is zero data in the database. The founder explicitly calls out P0: 'When the database is empty, show helpful empty states that guide the user to create their first item.' But the real problem is there IS no data at all. Without seed data no metric card shows a real number, no table has rows, and no workflow can be demonstrated.",
      "prompt": "Create the file supabase/seed.sql with comprehensive construction seed data. Include: 2 organizations (Turner Construction, Hensel Phelps), 3 projects (Riverside Commercial Tower $42M, Harbor View Mixed Use $28M, Metro Transit Hub $65M) with realistic addresses and dates. 10 users across roles (owner, admin, project_manager, superintendent, foreman, subcontractor, architect_engineer, inspector, read_only) with project_members rows linking them. 25 RFIs in various statuses (open, under_review, answered, closed) with realistic construction titles like 'Structural steel connection detail at grid line J-7', 'HVAC duct routing conflict in mechanical room 302'. 15 submittals (pending, approved, approved_as_noted, rejected, resubmit) with CSI spec sections. 50 punch_list_items across trades (electrical, drywall, painting, plumbing, HVAC) in all statuses. 30 daily_logs with weather data, crew counts, and work summaries spanning 30 consecutive days. Budget line items for 15 CSI divisions per project with original_budget, committed, spent_to_date, and projected values that sum correctly. 5 change_orders in different pipeline stages (draft, pending, approved, rejected, implemented) with realistic cost impacts. 10 schedule_phases with baseline dates, actual dates, percent_complete, dependencies, and critical path flags. 5 crews with trades and members. Activity feed entries for all the above. Notifications for each user. All UUIDs should be deterministic (use gen_random_uuid() or fixed UUIDs for test users matching the VERIFICATION_TESTS.md user table). All monetary values in integer cents. All dates within a realistic 18-month project timeline starting 2025-06-01. BEFORE: supabase/seed.sql does not exist. AFTER: Running `psql < supabase/seed.sql` populates all tables with rich, realistic construction data. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C5-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "useSupabase hooks missing for most entities — pages cannot query real data",
      "file": "src/hooks/useSupabase.ts",
      "description": "The PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P0 says 'Replace ALL mock data imports in pages with real useSupabase hooks.' The API layer in src/api/endpoints/ has functions but there are no unified React Query hooks in src/hooks/useSupabase.ts that pages can import. Pages need useRFIs, useSubmittals, useChangeOrders, usePunchList, useDailyLogs, useBudget, useSchedule, useCrew, useNotifications hooks with loading/error/refetch states and real-time subscriptions.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/useSupabase.ts, create a comprehensive set of React Query hooks that wrap the existing API endpoints. Import from '@tanstack/react-query' (useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient). Import the endpoint functions from '../api/endpoints/field', '../api/endpoints/documents', '../api/endpoints/people', '../api/endpoints/budget' (getCostData), and '../api/endpoints/projects'. Create these hooks: (1) useRFIs(projectId) — calls supabase.from('rfis').select('*').eq('project_id', projectId), returns {data, isLoading, error, refetch}, staleTime 30000. (2) useSubmittals(projectId) — same pattern for submittals. (3) usePunchList(projectId) — same for punch_list_items. (4) useDailyLogs(projectId) — same for daily_logs ordered by log_date desc. (5) useBudgetItems(projectId) — same for budget_items. (6) useSchedulePhases(projectId) — same for schedule_phases ordered by start_date. (7) useChangeOrders(projectId) — same for change_orders. (8) useCrews(projectId) — delegates to getCrews from people endpoint. (9) useNotifications(userId) — queries notifications table filtered by user_id, is_read=false, limit 20. Each hook should use queryKey pattern ['entity_name', projectId]. Each hook should set up a real-time subscription using supabase.channel() that invalidates the query on postgres_changes events. Export a useCreateMutation(tableName, projectId) generic helper that handles optimistic insert + rollback. BEFORE: No unified hook file exists for pages to consume. AFTER: Pages can import {useRFIs, useSubmittals, usePunchList, ...} and get typed, cached, real-time-subscribed data. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C5-003",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "performance",
      "title": "No cursor-based pagination — large datasets will freeze the UI",
      "file": "src/api/client.ts",
      "description": "The buildPaginatedQuery utility in src/api/client.ts uses offset-based pagination with .range(from, to). For tables with 500+ RFIs or 1000+ punch items, offset pagination degrades to O(n) on PostgreSQL. The API spec and backend architecture both mandate cursor-based pagination. The current implementation also lacks virtual scrolling integration. This means any project with realistic data volumes will have slow list pages.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/client.ts, add a new export function buildCursorPaginatedQuery that implements cursor-based pagination. BEFORE: Only buildPaginatedQuery exists using .range(from, to) offset pagination. AFTER: Add this function signature: export async function buildCursorPaginatedQuery<TRow, TMapped = TRow>(tableName: string, projectId: string, options: { pageSize?: number; cursor?: string; orderColumn?: string; orderAscending?: boolean; filterFn?: (query: any) => any; mapFn?: (row: TRow) => TMapped }): Promise<{ data: TMapped[]; nextCursor: string | null; hasMore: boolean }>. Implementation: (1) Build query with supabase.from(tableName).select('*').eq('project_id', projectId). (2) Apply filterFn if provided. (3) Order by options.orderColumn (default 'created_at') with ascending flag. (4) If cursor is provided, decode it from base64 to get the last seen value, then use .lt(orderColumn, decodedCursor) for descending or .gt for ascending. (5) Limit to pageSize + 1. (6) If results.length > pageSize, hasMore = true, slice to pageSize, encode last item's orderColumn value as base64 nextCursor. (7) Apply mapFn to each row. Also add a useInfiniteEntityQuery hook that wraps useInfiniteQuery from TanStack React Query using this cursor function: export function useInfiniteEntityQuery<T>(tableName: string, projectId: string, pageSize?: number). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C5-004",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "realtime",
      "title": "Real-time subscriptions not centralized — inconsistent channel management",
      "file": "src/hooks/useRealtimeSubscription.ts",
      "description": "The backend architecture specifies channel naming convention project:{uuid}:{table} and requires subscriptions on rfis, daily_logs, punch_list_items, notifications, activity_feed, schedule_activities, meetings, drawing_markups. The existing useRealtimeSubscription hook exists but individual page components do not consistently use it. There is no centralized subscription manager that batches channels per project, leading to potential duplicate subscriptions and missed cleanup on unmount.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/useRealtimeSubscription.ts, refactor the existing hook to add a centralized subscription registry and batch invalidation. BEFORE: The hook creates individual channels but has no deduplication, no registry tracking active subscriptions, and no batch invalidation pattern. AFTER: (1) Add a module-level Map<string, RealtimeChannel> called activeChannels that tracks all active subscriptions by channel name. (2) In the hook, before creating a new channel, check if activeChannels already has one for the same key — if so, reuse it and increment a reference count. (3) On cleanup, decrement the reference count and only unsubscribe when it reaches 0. (4) Add a new exported function subscribeToProjectTables(projectId: string, tables: string[], onUpdate: (table: string, payload: any) => void) that creates a single multiplexed channel 'project:{projectId}' listening to all specified tables via multiple .on() calls on the same channel. (5) Add a new exported hook useProjectRealtime(projectId: string) that subscribes to ['rfis', 'submittals', 'punch_list_items', 'daily_logs', 'change_orders', 'schedule_phases', 'notifications', 'activity_feed'] and calls queryClient.invalidateQueries with the matching queryKey when any change arrives. (6) Export a cleanup function unsubscribeAll() for use on logout. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C5-005",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "No RLS policies on 12+ tables created in migrations — data leaks across projects",
      "file": "supabase/migrations/20250402200000_add_missing_rls_policies.sql",
      "description": "The backend architecture mandates 'Every table has RLS enabled' and the auth spec says 'RLS is the security boundary. Client-side role checks are cosmetic.' However, multiple tables created in migrations (weather_records, equipment, equipment_logs, incidents, safety_observations, toolbox_talks, corrective_actions, time_entries, deliveries, material_inventory, warranties, warranty_claims, commissioning_items) likely lack RLS policies. Without RLS, any authenticated user can read/write data across all projects. This is a P0 security vulnerability per VERIFICATION_TESTS.md.",
      "prompt": "Create the file supabase/migrations/20250402200000_add_missing_rls_policies.sql with RLS policies for all tables that reference project_id but may lack proper row-level security. For each of these tables (weather_records, equipment, equipment_logs, equipment_maintenance, incidents, safety_observations, safety_inspections, toolbox_talks, toolbox_talk_attendees, corrective_actions, time_entries, deliveries, delivery_items, material_inventory, warranties, warranty_claims, commissioning_items, sustainability_metrics, waste_logs, photo_pins, photo_comparisons, progress_detection_results), add: (1) ALTER TABLE {table} ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; (only if not already enabled — use IF NOT EXISTS pattern or DO $$ block). (2) A SELECT policy: CREATE POLICY IF NOT EXISTS 'Project members can view' ON {table} FOR SELECT USING (project_id IN (SELECT pm.project_id FROM project_members pm WHERE pm.user_id = auth.uid())). (3) An INSERT policy: CREATE POLICY IF NOT EXISTS 'Project members can insert' ON {table} FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (project_id IN (SELECT pm.project_id FROM project_members pm WHERE pm.user_id = auth.uid())). (4) An UPDATE policy: same pattern. (5) A DELETE policy restricted to project_manager+ roles: CREATE POLICY IF NOT EXISTS 'Managers can delete' ON {table} FOR DELETE USING (project_id IN (SELECT pm.project_id FROM project_members pm WHERE pm.user_id = auth.uid() AND pm.role IN ('owner', 'admin', 'project_manager'))). Wrap everything in a DO $$ BEGIN ... EXCEPTION WHEN duplicate_object THEN NULL; END $$; block to be idempotent. BEFORE: Tables exist without RLS — any authenticated user can access all project data. AFTER: All project-scoped tables enforce row-level security based on project_members membership. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Automatic Query Performance Dashboard",
      "description": "A developer-facing page at /admin/query-performance that shows the 20 slowest Supabase queries from the last 24 hours, their execution plans, and suggested indexes. Uses pg_stat_statements extension already available in Supabase. Helps the team proactively prevent performance regressions as data grows.",
      "effort": "medium",
      "impact": "high"
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's API layer is battle-tested with 300+ integrations, cursor-based pagination, and webhook delivery guarantees. SiteSync's database layer has solid TypeScript types and error handling patterns but critically lacks seed data (making the entire app look empty), unified React Query hooks (forcing each page to roll its own data fetching), cursor pagination (which will break at scale), and complete RLS coverage (a security dealbreaker). Fieldwire's offline-first IndexedDB sync is far ahead. The API endpoint organization is clean and follows good patterns — the gap is in wiring those endpoints to the UI through standardized hooks and populating the database with realistic data.",
  "summary": "The Database & API Layer has a well-structured foundation: typed Supabase client, organized endpoint files, proper error transformation, project access middleware, and audit trail support. However, it is critically undermined by four gaps that make the entire application feel incomplete. First, there is no seed data whatsoever — every page shows empty states, every metric card shows zero, and no workflow can be demonstrated to a prospective GC customer. Second, there are no unified React Query hooks bridging the API endpoints to page components, forcing inconsistent data fetching patterns. Third, pagination is offset-based only, which will degrade badly with realistic data volumes (500+ RFIs per project is normal). Fourth, multiple tables lack RLS policies, creating cross-project data exposure — a P0 security vulnerability. The error handling and TypeScript patterns are above average, but without data to flow through them, they are untested infrastructure. Fixing seed data and hooks will produce the largest visible improvement across all pages simultaneously."
}
```
